channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Trump says Iran can call U.S. if it wants to negotiate
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi discussed security in the Strait of Hormuz with Oman officials. Iran FM back in Pakistan after Oman visit, says report; Iran's Foreign ​Minister Abbas Araghchi ‌held talks in ​Oman, discussed security ‌in the Strait of Hormuz, ‌broader ⁠Gulf waters, ⁠and diplomatic efforts to end the ​Iran-U. S.

item.title: Trump Event Shooter Mocked Hotel Security In Manifesto, Said 'Won't Use Slugs'
item.description: Trump stated the shooter acted alone as a "lone wolf". The suspect had mocked hotel security in a manifesto. Trump said officials believed he acted alone and was what he called a "lone wolf".

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah exchanged strikes in Lebanon as US-Iran talks stalled. Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister returns to Pakistan after U.S. envoys' trip canceled
item.description: Iran's foreign minister returned to Pakistan after a US envoy trip was canceled. Live Updates: Iran's foreign minister returns to Pakistan after U. S. envoys' trip canceled CBS News. Iran's foreign minister returns to Pakistan after U. S.

item.title: Mystery surrounds fate of detained journalist six months after fall of key Sudan city
item.description: Six months after the Rapid Support Forces captured Al Fashir, a detained journalist's fate remains unknown. Six months have passed since the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) captured the Sudanese regional capital Al Fashir and shared dozens of videos documenting their massacre in the city.

item.title: Gunman sought to kill Trump, cabinet at gala dinner
item.description: Cole Allen wrote a note ranking targets, including Trump and his cabinet, by priority. Publications have shared a note written by suspect Cole Allen that ranked potential targets from highest to lowest priority.

item.title: Champions-in-waiting Inter held to 2-2 draw as Torino fight back
item.description: Inter was held to a 2-2 draw by Torino after the opposing team fought back. Champions-in-waiting Inter held to 2-2 draw as Torino fight back.

item.title: Xi Jinping’s Four Peace Initiatives after the Iran War
item.description: Xi Jinping proposed Four Peace Initiatives after the Iran War. The initiatives were outlined in an article on Modern Diplomacy. Xi Jinping’s Four Peace Initiatives after the Iran War Modern Diplomacy.

item.title: Israel’s President, Putting Off Decision on Pardon for Netanyahu, Will Push for Plea Deal
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog will not pardon Benjamin Netanyahu. Instead, he will seek a plea deal in Netanyahu's corruption case. President Isaac Herzog of Israel has decided not to issue a pardon to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in his corruption case at this time, and instead will seek mediation, officials say.

item.title: Taiwan will not 'bow its head': FM hits out at China during visit to Eswatini
item.description: Taiwan's foreign minister said the country will not bow to China's pressure during a visit to Eswatini. “No unreasonable blockade or pressure can shake our resolve and will to safeguard our dignity and connect with the international community,” he added.

item.title: First trial of Assad-era Syrian official starts in Damascus
item.description: Syria's first public trial of a former Assad-era official, Atef Najib, has started in Damascus. Syria begun its first public trial of former Assad-era officials, with ex-security chief Atef Najib appearing in court.

item.title: UAE-Iran differences and India’s plans to dilute language on Israel-Palestine derail joint
item.description: India hosted a BRICS deputy foreign ministers' meeting, but a joint statement couldn't be agreed upon due to differences between UAE and Iran, and India on Israel-Palestine language. A 'Chair's Statement' was issued instead.

item.title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi Meets Asim Munir After Landing In Islamabad
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met with Pakistani army chief Asim Munir in Islamabad after visiting Oman. Araghchi arrived at Nur Khan Airbase in Rawalpindi. The Iranian leader arrived at Nur Khan Airbase in Rawalpindi after completing a day-long visit to Oman.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Sink Into Awkward Limbo of ‘No War, No Peace’
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi continued diplomatic efforts over the weekend, but US President Donald Trump scrapped a visit to Pakistan by his envoys, casting doubt on US-Iran peace hopes. The stalemate risks escalating tensions without a deal.

item.title: Russia, North Korea agree 'long-term' military cooperation
item.description: Russia and North Korea have agreed on long-term military cooperation, with a defense deal and memorial inauguration marking the agreement. Russia's defense minister and the chairman of the State Duma are both visiting North Korea.

item.title: Trump says Iran can call if it wants to talk, as Iranian envoy returns to Pakistan
item.description: US President Trump said Iran can contact him if it wants to talk, as the Iranian envoy returned to Pakistan after calling off planned negotiations. Trump offered to talk or meet.

item.title: Amid Iran's Attacks, UAE Called For Help. Israel Responded With Iron Dome
item.description: Israel deployed the Iron Dome to help the UAE counter Iran's attacks, which included 550 ballistic and cruise missiles and over 2,200 drones. The UAE had requested assistance. Iran struck the UAE with 550 ballistic and cruise missiles, and over 2,200 drones.

item.title: What to Know About King Charles and Queen Camilla’s U.S. State Visit Hosted by Trump
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla of Britain are set to visit the US for a state visit hosted by President Trump, with events including a garden party, a congressional address, and a banquet. The visit starts on Monday.

item.title: Colombia highway bomb attack kills at least 19
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced plans to expand Israel's control in Lebanon, ordering more evacuations in the region. The move comes as a ceasefire appears to be cracking, with violence escalating in the area.

item.title: Ceasefire cracking, Israel looks to spread area of control in Lebanon, orders more evacuations
item.description: Israel has ordered more evacuations in Lebanon as it seeks to expand its control in the region. The move follows reports of a ceasefire cracking, with violence escalating in the area.
